g.title: Base class for bearings and seals (dynamic coefficients)
Base class for seals. Inherits from BearingElement. Same dynamic coefficients but with sealing semantics instead of support (bearing).
kxx, kxy: direct and cross-coupled stiffness (x direction) kyx, kyy: cross-coupled and direct stiffness (y direction) Cross-coupled terms cause destabilizing forces (cross-coupled stiffness)
cxx, cxy: direct and cross-coupled damping (x) cyx, cyy: cross-coupled and direct damping (y) Direct damping stabilizes; cross-coupled can destabilize
Reference frequency [rad/s] Coefficients can vary with frequency. If array, interpolates for each speed.

Labyrinth seal - the most common in turbomachinery shaft_radius: shaft radius [m] radial_clearance: radial clearance [m] n_teeth: number of teeth pitch: tooth pitch [m] tooth_height: tooth height [m] seal_type: "see_through" or "interlocking" gas_composition: gas composition (dict) Calculates leakage and dynamic coefficients

Hole pattern seal shaft_radius: shaft radius [m] length: seal length [m] cell_length: cell length [m] cell_width: cell width [m] cell_depth: cell depth [m] Used in high-pressure centrifugal compressors Higher damping than labyrinth seals
Hybrid seal: combines labyrinth sections with hole-pattern sections. Aims to optimize sealing and stability.

Base class for seals. Inherits from BearingElement. Same dynamic coefficients but with sealing semantics instead of support (bearing).

Hybrid seal: combines labyrinth sections with hole-pattern sections. Aims to optimize sealing and stability.
